live in Denver, so time's typically a lot closer to everybody on your side.

So.

To.

the better this upfront investment of time and information that we get is, the better and quicker we'll get to a calibration on our articles, all the outputs of our workflows, and what that means is just better content quicker.

sorry to interrupt.

have VP of product marketing.

Yeah.
So, So,
N-able is historically known for being an RMM company.
The RMM products we have is how we got started.
And over the course of the past years, we've been acquiring other products.
Now we have a, this was probably six or seven years ago, but we acquired a data protection product.
We call it Cove, it's Backup and Data Protection.
Just in January, we acquired an MDR solution called AdLumen.
And our new CMO, Bikram, he was CMO at AdLumen, and he was actually getting ready to engage in a contract with you all.
And then we got acquired, he had to cut it on his end, but picked it back up over here.
So we are in the process of shifting from an IT security, IT management company to a technically cyber resiliency company is what we're calling it.
But think, you know, cyber resiliency.
cybersecurity.
So it's a huge, huge change in how we position the business.
And so there's a lot of education that needs to go in to that.
But also a lot of folks just from a product suite, know, a lot of prospects in our space, they still don't know about this data protection and backup product that we have.
You know, we've had it for seven years now or so.

So there's a lot of education to do around that on the global level, a ton of education to do on, you know, now we offer an MDR solution, brand new to the business and, you know, trying to sell that from an NCA perspective.
It's going to take a lot of thought leadership and a lot of education within our market.
So it's really, it's really the shift from, you know, an IT security, IT management to cyber resiliency, cybersecurity company.
I mean, it's
That makes sense.
Have you guys started with any of that shift, or is it kind of this engagement will be the beginning of that?
We've started, you know, the communications on our website.

Those are really, like, the big three that we lead with.
So, like, the framework is there.
We haven't, and it's on our website, we haven't done a ton of promotion outward.

then,
I guess what matters most to leadership now or kind of is there any pressure you guys are specifically feeling that, you know, we might be able to help with to say like in six months we nailed it?

came in well short of that in Q3.

Just bookings.

On the MDR front.

So, yeah, you know, we're probably going to ask you all to do a lot of security type of content for us that will be really focused on the MDR solution.

Then if we were to talk to three of your best customers, so not the biggest, but just best ones that maybe are kind of loyalists, what would they say you're uniquely good at from a larger enable kind of brand perspective?
Dang, that's probably a good question.
For some of our CSMs, or maybe some other folks on the marketing side, I don't do a ton of talking to our current customers.
I can tell you that our data protection and backup software, Cove, that one does really well once folks get their hands on it, and they can see how easy it is to backup.
It's cloud first, and you can have your first backups running in as little as 15 minutes.

Apparently, it's super easy to use, it's super fast, it's a newer cloud first solution versus some of the competitors are more image-based on-prem, not as fast and relies on, and there's some, oh, what's the word, forget it, forget that part.
But once folks, once people really get their hands on Cove, they love it, there's a great story to tell there.
Anyway, on the RMM side...
You know, we do position and central as being highly customizable.
That's for, you know, folks that have 10 or more users.
And it can be, you know, I think there's some good press there.
Again, this is probably where the product marketers will come in handy, a little bit stronger than me.
And on the MDR side, that's such new product for us, unfortunately.
I don't know a ton about really what sets us apart there quite yet.
Yep.

Yeah, I'd say any discussion with product marketing, and even if there's like, I don't know if you guys use Gong or any other sales conversations, we could tap into and probably just pry out some identifiers from those conversations.
But we can dig into that a little bit later.
And then for ICPs, are they generally a kind of core ICP that you have to identify, or are they multiple based off the different products?
Yeah, it's mainly a core ICP.
It's broken down into two.
So the first is MSPs, managed service providers, which I think anybody who is managing cybersecurity solutions for small and medium-sized businesses.
And we really break the MSPs down into two categories, three categories.
We have the 1 to 10 employee shops, the 11 to 50, and then the 50 plus.
And the 11 to 50 and 50 plus is really where we're trying to move.
We're trying to move up market.
You know, the solution, the trio of N-Central Cove and MDR is really built for these.
We typically, at least on the paid search front, we attract more of the one to tens, but I'm sure a lot of the content we're going to want to create here is going to be 11 to 15, 50 plus.
Those are our main segments there.
And then our other customer is what we classify as the mid-market enterprise, MME.
Think internal IT teams.
So IT teams at financial institutions, manufacturing facilities, healthcare facilities, education, those ones.
And I believe we want to target companies that have 250 plus product marketers will be able to fill us in a little bit bigger there, but that's a lot of.
Where we tend to be, 250 plus.
And if you were to split, like, percentage-wise, how much are MMEs versus MSPs as a current customer base?
Yeah, it's about 80-20 in favor of MSPs right now.
So really, the MME space is going to be where we really try to grow.
So we're going to try to move more into those 11 to 50 and 50-plus MSPs.
But really, the MME space is where we're trying to see – is where we're going to get our big growth from.
What is working about what you're trying to target MMEs currently for that 20%?
Paid LinkedIn.
That's really the bulk of what we do, at least on my team.
You know, we run some webinars, we run some events, but, you know, we're reaching maybe 100.
The 200 people, when we do those things, paid LinkedIn, we can reach them at scale.
We use a tool called metadata.io that really helps us just scaling our paid social program.
And we spend probably about, I want to say, two and a half million every year on that channel right now.
We want to spend more.
We want to double that in 2026, at that was the proposal.
So we put in, so we'll see if that gets approved.
Really, paid social program is the bulk of how we're reaching them right now.
Makes sense.

I mean, you have SEO people on the team and some other teams that sound like they work in parallel.

So, you know, going that new something, or like prospecting that new prospecting is something we've kind of dabbled in with the blog, but one, we're not good at it, and two, we haven't done it at scale quite yet.
We're doing, this is even really MSP, so no, I really wouldn't say that's an MME type deal.

mean, SentinelOne is one of them.

We have local German servers.
It's all still housed within the local country.
You know, it's not housed somewhere over here randomly in the U.S.
Local servers is how we put around that.
Because the same thing in Australia, you know, we needed to have a local data center there.

sounds like we already kind of had that lined up.

Where would you say best leads or users are coming from today?
that, I guess, we're talking about new users or?
Yeah, most of, 80% of our marketing lead, or of our leads come from what we classify as core MGen, paid search, organic, web direct.
We've been really, really heavily focused as an organization.
You know, we spun out, we used to be part of SolarWinds.
We spun off from SolarWinds in June of 2021.
And we have just been so bottom of funnel focused since then.
We, I don't want to say we've completely avoided the top of funnel awareness, creating demand type piece.
But we are, you know, our NCA motion is predominantly focused on paid search.
And as we know, paid search is great for capturing demand.
It's not great at creating that demand.
So, hence, again, while we're partnering with somebody like yourselves to come in and help that more top of funnel education type piece.

Like conferences, events?
Conferences, events, yeah.
Outbound sales calling.

It's pretty even, to be honest with you.

right.

okay.

What we classify as qualified opportunities.
Qualified apps.

We do have a big part of what we'll want to attack in 2026 is channel.
So resellers, a lot of the MME, a lot of folks in the MME space will buy from resellers rather than directly through vendors themselves.
And that's particularly big in Europe.
So we'll want to have some campaigns that specifically target the resellers as well.
And when you say campaigns, that, you mean like content campaigns or do you guys, are you talking about this paid search?
Yeah, I mean, campaigns across the board, we've been really bad about full funnel campaigns in the past.
We were not structured to support full funnel campaigns and then new CMO came on and we're much more structurally aligned to do that.
So everything from content to paid social.
And then if we take a step and just kind of look at the current state of marketing overall, what would you say is working versus not working?
It sounds like paid search is...
Yeah, paid search is definitely working.
I'd say paid social is also working.
We need to get better at nurturing our opportunities down the funnel.
We are creating a ton of leads on the paid social side of things, but our nurture emails are not demand gen nurture emails.
They are much more built around keeping folks warm and just up to date on the company rather than having a specific call to action that helps keep moving them down the funnel a little bit more.
There's a big belief that we have what's called our enterprise team, and that team is really focused on the 11 to 50 and 50 plus segments of the MSPs.
And what they do is they get a bunch of MSPs together for like basically roundtables, lunch and learns type deals where they get them all in the same room.
And we typically take them out to some sort of dinner or event, some sort of sporting event or something like that afterwards.
Big belief that those are really working hard on those, really working well on that 11 to 50, 50 plus segment on the MSP side.
More like enterprise events and in-person.
Correct.
Yeah.
Social.
Yeah.

think we're in a good spot.

Then for competing priorities, is that similar vein as the other kind of too many cooks?

We, yeah, just, we're just a huge company, man.
We have, we got 1,900 folks here across the globe.

you.

Yeah, I'd say ICP lead generation would be a big one.
know, one thing, maybe this is, sorry, an area that's not working.

So ICP lead generation, bookings.

More often, how do we make that a bigger focus?

yeah, you too.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Kickoff meeting to align on N-able's goals and needs for GrowthX engagement, focusing on content strategy to support shift to cyber resiliency positioning.](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=474.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [N-able is transitioning from IT management to cyber resiliency, requiring significant market education](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=474.0)
  - [Key growth focus is on MDR product and mid-market enterprise (MME) segment](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [Current marketing efforts heavily bottom-funnel; need to develop more top-funnel awareness content](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=2012.0)
  - [Approval processes and competing priorities are major bottlenecks in content production](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=2571.0)

## Topics

### Company Transition and Growth Focus

  - [Shifting from IT management to cyber resiliency/security positioning](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=474.0)
  - [Three main products: N-Central (RMM/UEM), Cove (Data Protection), AdLumen (MDR)](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=614.0)
  - [Primary growth focus on MDR product and mid-market enterprise (MME) segment](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [Current revenue split: 80% MSPs, 20% MME; aiming to grow MME significantly](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=1024.0)

### Target Audience and ICP

  - [MSPs: 1-10 employees, 11-50 employees, 50+ employees (focus on 11-50 and 50+)](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=904.0)
  - [MME: Internal IT teams, 250+ employees (financial, manufacturing, healthcare, education)](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=981.0)
  - [Resellers: Important channel, especially in Europe](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=2211.0)

### Current Marketing Efforts

  - [80% of leads from "core MGen": paid search, organic, web direct](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=2012.0)
  - [Paid social (LinkedIn) working well, spending \~$2.5M annually](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=1061.0)
  - [Enterprise team focuses on in-person events for 11-50 and 50+ MSP segments](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=2347.0)
  - [Top-performing content: SOC report, threat report, backup guides, free scripts](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=2412.0)

### Challenges and Bottlenecks

  - [Approval processes often too long with "too many cooks in the kitchen"](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=2571.0)
  - [Competing priorities across large organization (1,900 employees globally)](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=2571.0)
  - [Limited scalability in content creation and distribution](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=2571.0)
  - [Lack of effective nurture campaigns for moving leads down the funnel](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=2283.0)

### Competitors and Differentiation

  - [RMM/UEM: Ninja1, Atera, Kaseya, ConnectWise](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=1497.0)
  - [Data Protection: Veeam, Acronis, Axcient](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=1567.0)
  - [MDR: Huntress, Arctic Wolf, Red Canary (600+ providers in market)](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=1610.0)
  - [N-able differentiation: Cloud-first approach, local data centers, "head nerds" for customer support](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=1733.0)

### Desired Outcomes and Metrics

  - [Increased ICP lead generation and web traffic](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=2718.0)
  - [Improved domain authority and AI search visibility](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=2718.0)
  - [Consistent presence in top 3 vendor shortlists](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=2825.0)
  - [Overall bookings growth, especially for MDR product](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Schedule deep-dive meetings with Product Marketing, Customer Success, and Brand teams](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=2917.0)
  - [GrowthX to create writing guidelines, audience personas, and company context artifacts](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=3084.0)
  - [Develop high-level Topic Cluster Content Strategy](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=3084.0)
  - [Present content strategy and Atlas platform demo in next week's meeting](https://fathom.video/share/eckauVVHGNWqWLiTmwGhdxBSsyNdUpXK?tab=summary&timestamp=3084.0)

action_items.description: Schedule deep dive meetings w/ PMM, customer success, brand team. Check calendars, propose times to GrowthX.
Create email thread w/ all relevant team members for GrowthX to follow up individually.
Create writing guidelines, audience personas, company context artifact as inputs for Atlas platform.
Prepare demo of Atlas content generation platform for next week's meeting.
Develop high-level Topic Cluster Content Strategy to present next week.
